title: Senior NIH official pushes MAHA strategy to skeptical ADA audience
summary: An NIH advisor told researchers gathered at the American Diabetes Association conference that MAHA goals match the agency's priorities.

title: Study data convince Lundbeck to push new migraine drug forward
summary: A possible alternative to “anti-CGRP” medications, the drug didn’t meet some analysts’ expectations in mid-stage testing but still convinced Lundbeck to pursue further development.

title: Generic drugmakers gain key victory in ‘skinny label’ patent case
summary: The Supreme Court ruled that a knockoff version of Amarin’s Vascepa didn’t infringe on patents — a decision with far-reaching implications for the availability of generics.

title: Opinion: If Americans risk their lives fighting Ebola overseas, they deserve the right to come home
summary: Outbreak response is difficult enough without asking responders to wonder whether they will have access to the best available care if they become ill.

summary: While many drugmakers are focused on lung cancer, evidence shows the liver may be a better bet.

title: Tiny HHS office tasked with protecting research participants’ safety is running on fumes
summary: OHRP has experienced an unprecedented exodus of ethics expertise, raising concerns about its ability to effectively protect volunteers.

title: Opinion: How living and practicing medicine abroad complicated my beliefs about universal health care systems
summary: “I believe deeply in universal health care. But universal care must also mean universal safety and accountability, not merely universal access.”

title: HHS confirms Americans with high-risk Ebola exposures will have access to experimental therapy
summary: Americans who have high-risk exposures to Ebola in the current outbreak in Central Africa will have access to an experimental antibody treatment.

title: STAT+: Drug companies, patient groups urge FDA to pause commissioner’s voucher program
summary: The White House has used the program to reward companies that help its political goals.

title: What RevMed’s pancreatic cancer drug meant for one patient
summary: Why did a presentation on Revolution Medicines' pancreatic cancer drug get a standing ovation? Why did biotech stocks perform so badly this week? Find out on "The Readout LOUD."

title: STAT+: Otsuka kidney drug slowed loss of function, but less than expected, in late-stage trial
summary: Otsuka’s Voyxact slowed the loss of kidney function after one year in patients with a chronic autoimmune kidney disease, but the benefit was less than expected.

title: STAT+: Supreme Court backs generic drugmaker in ‘skinny labeling’ case
summary: The Supreme Court unanimously ruled that Hikma Pharmaceutical did not infringe patents held by Amarin in a decision with implications for lawsuits over so-called skinny labels.

summary: For pancreatic cancer patients, the surge in interest for an exciting new drug is leaving them fearful about when they might get the treatment or if there will be enough…

summary: Assessing the health secretary's biggest challenges, and a look at new rules that inject politics into the NIH.

summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: An update on the Utah pilot that uses a chatbot to renew drug prescriptions, AI scribes for patients, and more.

title: An ADC specialist sinks on safety concerns for blood cancer drug
summary: Shares for ADC Therapeutics fell more than 50% after a Phase 3 trial observed a death rate that at least one analyst believes “may be difficult to accept for physicians, patients and regulators.”

title: Otsuka showcases kidney drug data; Pfizer cuts an AI deal

summary: Revolution Medicines' pancreatic cancer drug has not yet been approved — and already talk has shifted to how (and when) its use might be expanded.

title: A Spanish biotech banks $87M to make differentiated ADCs
summary: Ona Therapeutics, a Barcelona-based drugmaker, is developing next-generation drugs it says could help patients with advanced forms of breast and colorectal cancers.

title: Therapeutic and prophylactic strategies for Ebola caused by Bundibugyo virus
Legend surges on early data for ‘in vivo’ lymphoma cell therapy
summary: Initial findings show that the therapy reduced or eliminated disease signs in all recipients, sparking hopes that the treatment might be competitive with “ex vivo” medicines like Novartis’ Kymriah.

title: House bill aims to crack down on China biotech deals
summary: A proposal to add biotechnology to the COINS Act follows two big deals involving Pfizer and Bristol Myers, and comes amid U.S. fears that China’s drugmaking progress may threaten national security.

title: NewLimit’s $435M megaround; Lilly’s busy dealmaking week
summary: The anti-aging biotech has secured one of the year’s biggest venture fundings to bring its first drug into testing. Elsewhere, Lilly nabbed a “GLP-2” obesity drug and Praxis shares tumbled.
